Best Regards

LakshmiNarayana

entry.title: TDS Configuration and deduction under Section 194C
entry.content:
Hi experts!

I’m configuring India TDS 194C (contractor payments) in S/4HANA Extended Withholding Tax and need guidance on the annual aggregate threshold behavior.

Requirement (194C thresholds):

  • Deduct TDS if any single payment/credit exceeds ₹30,000, or
  • Deduct TDS if aggregate payments/credits to the same contractor in a financial year exceed ₹1,00,000.

What I’ve done / issue:

  • I can set a minimum amount = ₹30,000 in the WHT code, which correctly triggers TDS on a single invoice of ₹35,000.
  • However, this minimum-per-document setting seems to prevent TDS from being calculated on smaller invoices even after the vendor’s FY cumulative crosses ₹1,00,000, and I can’t find where to configure the ₹1,00,000 annual accumulation/threshold.

 

Example scenario (each invoice < ₹30,000):

  • Apr: ₹25,000 (cumulative ₹25,000) – no TDS
  • Jun: ₹25,000 (cumulative ₹50,000) – no TDS
  • Aug: ₹25,000 (cumulative ₹75,000) – no TDS
  • Oct: ₹25,000 (cumulative ₹1,00,000) – still no TDS (threshold is “exceeds”)
  • Dec: ₹20,000 (cumulative ₹1,20,000) – TDS should become applicable once the aggregate exceeds ₹1,00,000, but with the ₹30,000 minimum set SAP still doesn’t withhold.

 

Questions:

  1. In standard Extended Withholding Tax, where do we maintain the annual exemption/threshold limit (₹1,00,000) with accumulation?
  2. Can standard config support the “single-sum OR annual-aggregate” logic together (i.e., still withhold on the Dec ₹20,000 once cumulative exceeds ₹1,00,000), or does this require an enhancement/BAdI?

Thanks, happy to share screenshots of our current WHT type/code settings if helpful.

Kat

entry.title: Re: SideEffect is not refreshing the criticality icon after bound action call
entry.content:
Hi,

i think you are missing one line related to CriticalityRepresentation in your annotation.cds file. Please Try to add that line and check. BTW, your cap videos on youtube are really helpful 🙂

   $Type : 'UI.DataField',
                Value : status_code,
                Label : 'Availibility',
                Criticality : status.criticality,
                CriticalityRepresentation : #WithIcon,

entry.title: In SAP community I'm unable to format my 'comments' in responses.
entry.content:
Detailed Description:
I have posted in both the SAP user community section and in the SAP partner community an issue I was encountering for a customer.  I have received responses from contributors, but when I attempted to respond to these, I am unable to format comments in responses.  That is, the comment section is one long text string and does not give me the ability to enter paragraphs or force a next line entry.
Can this be checked please.  Is it a permissions issue?
 
Steps to Reproduce:
Please see the example below
SAP user community post
CLM - Contract and Lease Management Migration in P... - SAP Community
You can see my comment response on this one, where it's come through as one long string of text.
The contributor does not seem to have this problem as their response back is formatted ok.
 
Thanks
Sonya

entry.title: Picking Number Range Configuration by Organizational Unit - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We would like to request clarification regarding the configuration possibilities for picking document number ranges in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Business Scenario:
Our organization operates multiple sales organizations with different company codes and plants. For internal tracking and audit purposes, the business would like the picking document numbers to reflect the organizational unit that created the document. For example:

  • Picking documents created in one company code should follow a specific number range (e.g., XXXXX–XXXXX).
  • Picking documents created in another company code should follow a different number range (e.g., YYYYY–YYYYY).

Please let us know if it is possible with flexible numbering as well.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Picking Number Range Configuration by Organizational...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

I hope you are doing well.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, number ranges for logistics execution documents, including picking and warehouse-related documents, are not designed to be determined by organizational units such as Company Code, Plant, or Sales Organization. Instead, they are assigned at document type level, and there is no standard configuration or extensibility available to influence this dynamically based on organizational data.

Flexible Numbering is limited to selected business objects and does not cover warehouse or picking documents. In addition, number range settings are restricted to predefined configuration steps (SSCUI), without support for conditional logic based on organizational attributes for this type of document.

In practice, this requirement is usually addressed through alternative approaches. For example, the relevant organizational unit may be captured separately, or the relationship may be handled at reporting level. Standard analytical tools can be used to combine picking documents with delivery and organizational data, such as plant and Company Code, allowing traceability without changing the document numbering logic.

If it is essential for the organizational unit to be reflected directly in the document number, this would require a product enhancement, as this capability is not available in the current standard functionality. In such cases, you may submit an improvement request via the SAP Customer Influence portal, where enhancement requests can be proposed and voted on by other customers.

For guidance, please refer to SAP Note:
3206294- How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management:
https://me.sap.com/notes/3206294/en-US

Regards,
Leticia

entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALMを申請したお客様向けウェルカムセッションのご案内 (日本語開催)

このセッションでは、SAP Cloud ALMのコンセプトや機能概要と合わせ、SAP Cloud ALM プロビジョニング後に必要となるSAP Cloud ALMの初回アクセス方法や必要となる初期設定方法についても説明いたします。
SAP Cloud ALMの概要をまず押さえたい方やSAP Cloud ALMのプロビジョニングが完了したものの、その後の利用準備に至っていない方など、SAP Cloud ALMの活用に向けてスタートしたいお客様、パートナー様にとって最適なセッションです。

 

アジェンダ：

·         SAP Cloud ALMへの初回アクセス方法

·         ユーザの追加と初期設定の方法

·         機能概要のご紹介

·         役立つ資料やサイトのご案内

·         Q&A

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM 환영 웨비나 | 대한민국
entry.content:
안녕하세요 고객 여러분,

SAP Cloud ALM을 사용하기로 결정하신 것을 기쁘게 생각합니다. SAP Cloud ALM 을 사용하기 위한 처음 단계에 필요한 모든 중요 정보가 들어 있는 “Access information for SAP Cloud ALM” 이메일을 최근에 받으셨을 것입니다.

더불어서 SAP는 처음으로 SAP를 사용하기 시작한 고객에게 실시간으로 직접적인 지원을 제공 하고자 합니다. 따라서 귀하와 모든 신규 SAP Cloud ALM 고객을 위해 18 December 에 아래 내용이 포함된 웹세션을 제공할 예정 입니다.

 

• SAP Cloud ALM에 처음 로그인하는 단계
• 첫 번째 로그인 후 일반적인 다음 단계에 대한 설명
• 고객을 위한 필수 SAP Cloud ALM 학습 자료를 찾는 방법
• 알아두면 도움이 되는 잠재적인 문제에 대한 팁
• 참석 고객의 질문에 대한 답변 및 안내 제공

Register Now

entry.title: Re: Create new user
entry.content:
I understand you could not create new S-user for *

Regarding to your issue, please kindly note that only user admin/cloud admin/super admin could create S-user.

Since your S-user S0026121735 is not an admin user, you do not have authorizations to create new S-user for others.

In SAP for Me, the My Important Contacts page will display a list of the Super Administrators, Cloud Administrators, and User Administrators for your customer number.

Please kindly contact your admin to help create a new S-user. If you need admin role also, please contact your super admin to assign relevant authorizations to your S-user.

Relevant KBAs:

  • 1271482 - How does an administrator create or delete S-user IDs? - SAP for Me 
  • 1511008 - How to add or change authorizations for a S-User ID - SAP for Me
  • 1282821 - How to determine if my S-User ID is a User, Super, or Cloud Administrator - SAP For Me

entry.title: Re: Add metadata in SAP BPA Mail Body
entry.content:
Hello @RomnickJohn ,

You can’t append content directly within the send mail activity in BPA. Instead, use a script task to append the information, then map the resulting URL or content to the email—this approach will work.

entry.title: Re: TDS Configuration and deduction under Section 194C
entry.content:
Answer to your questions:

  1. Annual exemption/threshold limit (₹1,00,000) cannot be maintained in standard config; only per-document minimum is available.
  2. Standard cannot support “single-sum OR annual-aggregate” together; you need an enhancement (via BAdI/user exit) to implement the cumulative threshold logic. You can try with - BAdI FIBL_WT_TAX_CALC or BADI_J_1IEWT_ACCUMULATION which are commonly used to implement cumulative threshold checks for Indian TDS.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Account Determination of OBYC - GBB (VAX/VA...
entry.content:
Why you see only VAY is because, in Public Cloud, if you are using MM-based intercompany procurement, the system posts the payable side (VAY). VAX is tied to SD-based intercompany sales (billing documents). If you haven’t executed an intercompany billing (IV invoice), VAX won’t trigger.

Configure valuation classes/account modifiers in OBYC to differentiate intercompany postings.

  • VAX = selling company revenue (SD intercompany billing).
  • VAY = buying company payable (MM intercompany procurement).

In Public Cloud, you will see VAY more often because IC procurement is MM-driven, not SD-driven.

entry.title: Re: How to connect to SAP HANA using VSCode SQL Tool Sett...
entry.content:
Guide: Connecting SAP HANA to VS Code via SQLTools

Integrating your SAP HANA database with Visual Studio Code allows for a seamless development experience. Follow this structured guide to configure your connection using the SQLTools extension and the SAP HANA Driver.

Prerequisites

Before starting, ensure you have the SQLTools extension installed in VS Code.

Step 1: Provision SAP HANA Service Instance

To establish a connection, you must first generate the necessary credentials within the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) cockpit.

  1. Navigate to your Subaccount and go to Instances and Subscriptions.

  2. Create a new instance of SAP HANA Schemas & HDI Containers.

  3. Once the instance is created, generate a Service Key.

  4. Open the Service Key and locate the following parameters:

    • host

    • port (typically 443)

    • user (or hdi_user)

    • password (or hdi_password)

      Step 2: Install the SAP HANA Driver

      To enable HANA support within SQLTools, you must install the specific driver:

      1. Open the VS Code Marketplace.

      2. Search for and install the SAP HANA Driver for SQLTools.

      Step 3: Configure the Connection Assistant

      Once the driver is active, you can link your database through the SQLTools interface.

      1. Open the SQLTools icon in the activity bar and click on Add New Connection.

      2. Select the SAP HANA tile to launch the Connection Assistant.

      3. Fill in the following fields using the values from your Service Key:

      4. FieldValue / Description
        Connection nameA unique identifier for your project.
        HANA ServerEnter the host value from your Service Key.
        Server PortEnter 443.
        SchemaEnter the hdi_user value (standard HDI schemas match the username).
        UsernameEnter the hdi_user value.
        PasswordEnter the hdi_password value.

        Step 4: Validate and Connect

        1. Click Test Connection to ensure the credentials and network path are valid.

        2. If successful, click Save Connection.

        3. Locate your new connection in the sidebar and click Connect.

          Results

          Upon a successful connection, the SQLTools sidebar will populate with your database catalog, allowing you to browse tables, views, and metadata. A Session SQL tab will open automatically, enabling you to execute queries and perform local data testing directly within VS Code.

entry.title: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 26.3
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I'm happy to announce that a new release of the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) is now available for all SAP Build and Mobile Services customers. You can download it from the SAP Software Center, and it will soon be available on the community download page.

The SAP Mobile Services Client has been updated to version 26.3.0 on the Apple App Store and the Google Play Store.

SAP MDK enables developers and technical business users to build cross-platform mobile applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment (SAP Business Application Studio / Visual Studio Code) and run it natively on Mobile (iOS & Android).  

Below UI enhancements are available only on iOS and Android platforms:

  • Support Styling of Key Value Item Control
  • Contact Cell Control Enhancements
  • Support In-App Camera in FormCell Attachment Control (Android Only)
  • New ActionBar Caption Size Options
  • Support Required Field Indicator for Segmented FormCell Control
  • Support Uploading Offline Store to Mobile Services
  • Onboarding via MDM App Config (Managed Configuration)

Support Styling of Key Value Item Control

MDK now supports styling Key Value item control. A new object property Styles has been introduced with following sub-properties:

  • KeyValueItem: supports only background color to style entire item.
  • KeyName: supports to style key name.
  • Value: supports to style a value property.
//excerpt of an MDK page
"KeyAndValues": [{
        "Value": "{City}",
        "_Type": "KeyValue.Type.Item",
        "_Name": "KeyValue0",
        "KeyName": "City",
        "Visible": true,
        "OnPress": "/MDK263/Actions/UpdateEntitySuccessMessage.action",
        "LinkColor": "#0000FF",
        "Styles": {
            "KeyValueItem": "KeyValueItemBackground",
            "KeyName": "KeyNameStyle",
            "Value": "KeyValueStyle"
        }
    },

//excerpt of a .less file 
.KeyValueItemBackground {
    background-color: #FFA500;
}
.KeyNameStyle {
    font-color: #ADD8E6;
    text-decoration: underline;
}
.KeyValueStyle {
    font-color: green;
}

Note:  : Value  style will take priority over LinkColor, regardless of whether OnPress is defined or not.

 

Key Value Styling on iOS and Andrid devices

 For more information, refer to the Key Value Item documentation.

Contact Cell Control Enhancements

  • Support for Initials: The ContactCell component now supports displaying user initials, providing a more personalized and recognizable visual experience when profile pictures are unavailable. This enhancement makes it easier to scan and identify contacts by replacing generic placeholder icons with meaningful initials. Three new properties have been introduced to support this feature:
    • DetailImageText: A string property that displays text in the image container when no DetailImage is present. It supports a maximum of 2–4 characters, allowing you to manually specify custom text such as initials.
    • DetailImageIsCircular: A boolean property with a default value of true that controls the shape of the image container. When set to true, the image is displayed in a circular shape; when set to false, it is displayed in a rounded rectangular shape.
    • UseHeadlineForDetailImage: A boolean property with a default value of false. When set to true and no DetailImage is provided, the control automatically extracts and displays the first two initials from the Headline property in the image container. This property takes precedence over DetailImageText.
  • Support for BadgeImage Property:  A new property, BadgeImage, is now available on ContactCell. This property allows you to attach an additional indicator icon or image to the existing DetailImage or DetailImageText for each contact. For example, it can be used to highlight and identify primary contacts in a contact list. Additionally, the existing Styles property for ContactCellItem now supports a new subproperty named BadgeImage, which enables CSS styling for the indicator icon or image defined in the BadgeImage property.
//excerpt of .page
{
    "ContactCell": {
        "_Name": "ContactCellItem6",
        "BadgeImage": "sap-icon://bell",
        "DetailImageText": "SB",
        "DetailImageIsCircular": true,
        "Headline": "Sue Bay",
        "Subheadline": "Antioch, Illinois",
        "UseHeadlineForDetailImage": false,
        "Visible": true,
        "ActivityItems": [{
                "_Name": "ContactCellItem4ActivityItems0",
                "ActivityType": "Phone"
            }
        ],
        "Styles": {
            "DetailImage": "DetailImage",
            "BadgeImage": "BadgeImage"
        }
    }
},

//excerpt of Styles.less
.BadgeImage {
    color: #068831;
    background-color: #ffae00;
}
.DetailImage {
    color: #AD00FF;
    background-color: #ADD8E6;
}

Contact Cell Control on iOS and Android devices

For more information, refer to the Contact Cell documentation.

Support In-App Camera in FormCell Attachment Control (Android Only)

We have received feedback from customers about the MDK app restarting after taking a photo using the Attachment control, especially when the app is running on Android devices with lower RAM. This may be due to memory pressure on the device, which can cause the OS to kill the MDK app running on background after launching the camera.

To address this problem, we are providing support for using an in-app camera option. This option consumes less memory, and the camera opens directly inside the MDK app instead of switching to a separate camera application.

  • A new UseInAppCamera property is available in the Application.app file under the Settings object. It determines whether the in-app camera or the device's default camera will be used for the AttachmentFormCell control’s “Take Photo” option.
    • When set to true, the in-app camera will be used instead of launching the device’s native camera application.
//excerpt of Application.app
{
    "MainPage": "/MDK263/Pages/Main.page",
    "OnLaunch": [
        "/MDK263/Rules/Service/Initialize.js"
    ],
    "Settings": {
        "UseInAppCamera": true
    },
    "_Name": "MDK263"
}

In addition, we are introducing two new APIs to manage the in-app camera settings programmatically:

  • getUseInAppCamera 
    • Retrieves the current state of the in-app camera usage flag.
    • Returns the current value determining whether the in-app camera is enabled for FormCell Attachment controls.
  • setUseInAppCamera 
    • Enables or disables the in-app camera for FormCell Attachment controls
    • Changes take effect immediately for new AttachmentFormCell instances
    • For AttachmentFormCell controls that are already rendered on the current page, the new setting will only take effect after calling the control’s reset() API. Calling the reset() API will discard any existing attachments in the control.
export default function ChangeUseInAppCamera(context) {
  let currentValue = context.getUseInAppCamera();
  context.setUseInAppCamera(!currentValue);
  alert('UseInAppCamera set to ' + !currentValue);
  let currentPageProxy = context.getPageProxy();
  if (currentPageProxy) {
    if (currentPageProxy.getName() === 'FCMain') {
      let attachmentControlProxy = context.evaluateTargetPathForAPI('#Page:FCMain/#Control:FormCellAttachment0');
      if (attachmentControlProxy) {
        attachmentControlProxy.reset();
      }
    }
  }
}

New ActionBar Caption Size Options

The ActionBar now supports flexible caption sizing through the new PreferredCaptionSize property, which replaces the existing PrefersLargeCaption property.

  • Enhanced size control: Choose from small, medium, or large caption sizes instead of toggling between only two options.
    • iOS supports small and large caption size
    • Android supports small, medium, and large caption size.

Behavior Notes:        

  • When PreferredCaptionSize is set to "large", any defined Subhead will be ignored.
  • When setting CaptionAlignment (Android only) to “center”, the PreferCaptionSize automatically set to small.  
  • On iOS, large caption mode is not supported on pages with BottomNavigation, Tabs, SideDrawer, FormCellContainer, or FlexibleColumn controls.
  • If both PreferredCaptionSize and the deprecated PrefersLargeCaption property are specified, PreferredCaptionSize takes precedence.
//excerpt of an MDK page 
"ActionBar": {
        "Items": [
            {
                "_Type": "Control.Type.ActionBarItem",
                "_Name": "ActionBarItem0",
                "Caption": "User Menu",
                "OnPress": "/MDK263/Actions/Application/UserMenuPopover.action"
            }
        ],
        "_Name": "ActionBar1",
        "_Type": "Control.Type.ActionBar",
        "Caption": "MDK 26.3",
        "PreferredCaptionSize": "Medium"
    }
}

 

Support Required Field Indicator  for Segmented FormCell Control 

In MDK 25.9 release, we introduced support for required field indicators for some FormCell controls. In this release, we are extending this support to the Segmented FormCell, indicating that the field must be filled out.
Note: This feature does not enforce users to fill out the form cell; it only provides a visual indicator that the field is required.

//Excerpt from a .page
{
    "RequiredIndicator": true,
    "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SegmentedControl",
    "_Name": "PrioritySegmented",
    "Caption": "Priority",
    "ApportionsSegmentWidthsByContent": false,
    "Segments": [
        "Low",
        "Medium",
        "High"
    ]
}

Form Cell Segmented control on iOS and Android devices

 

Support Uploading Offline Store to Mobile Services 

MDK now provides the ability for applications to upload the offline store (Both the main offline database (UDB) and the Request Queue database) to Mobile Services to assist with troubleshooting. This capability helps developers and support teams more effectively investigate and diagnose issues related to the offline store, reducing the time required to identify the root cause of problems reported by business users.
To facilitate this capability, a new MDK action has been introduced: Action.Type.OfflineOData.UploadStore.

{
    "_Type": "Action.Type.OfflineOData.UploadStore",
    "ActionResult": {
        "_Name": "UploadOfflineDB"
    },
    "Service": "/MDK263/Services/SampleServiceV4.service",
    "EncryptionKey": "MDK123", // encryption key used during the upload process
    "Note": "My Test App" // Optional note attached to the uploaded database file
}

In order to use this feature, you will need to first enable offline store upload policy in SAP Mobile Services Admin UI for a given MDK app. Developers can download the uploaded files on their local machine and use ILODATA tool (link to doc) to analyze them.  (link how to use this tool). For more information, see this  documentation.

Onboarding via MDM App Config (Managed Configuration)

MDK now supports onboarding via MDM App Config (Managed Configuration). Company administrators can push onboarding settings directly to managed devices through their MDM solution.

The MDK client retrieves MDK-defined configurations from a reserved root property named MDKSettings within the Managed Configuration. Currently, this root property only accepts the ConnectionSettings object. The supported properties, their functionality, and the required structure for ConnectionSettings remain the same as in the existing implementation.

While the iOS and Android versions differ syntactically due to their file formats, they share the same structural hierarchy. Note that the actual syntax may vary depending on the requirements of the specific MDM solution being used.

  • iOS: MDM solutions for iOS commonly use an XML format for complex configurations.
  • Android: MDM solutions for Android commonly use a JSON format for complex configurations.

Become an SAP MDK Explorer!
Do you have a requirement to build a mobile solution for your enterprise? Take your first steps today by following these tutorials to get started and learn more about the SAP Mobile development kit. We’re excited to see what you build—your feedback and ideas are invaluable!

Submit Improvement Requests
You can submit your improvement request (link) for Mobile development kit to the SAP Customer Influence site, a central place for all product improvement requests. You can browse all improvement requests, submit improvement requests, comment, vote, receive updates, and see who has voted at the SAP Customer Influence site.

entry.title: Re: Temporary Protected Status Extension
entry.content:
@jphaney , this could be recorded during Form I-9 Section 1 in the event that the New Hire Document Upload control is enabled. The New Hire has an opportunity to enter the appropriate Expiration Date here.

In addition, during Section 2 (and possibly 3), the proper Expiration Date can be entered. There is also a text field for the "Additional Notes" which is where any Notes related to this Form I-9 would be recorded.

entry.title: Re: SLED mandatory check ignored in Fiori App F1043 (Post...
entry.content:
Why the behavior differs is because

MIGO (GUI) fully respects the movement type setting (“1 = Enter Expiration Date”) and the material master’s shelf life fields. With Total Shelf Life = 0 and Min. Rem. Shelf Life > 0, the system forces manual entry of the SLED.

In Fiori F1043, the app is built on newer APIs and does not enforce the same mandatory check when Total Shelf Life = 0. It only makes the field mandatory if a production date is entered or if total shelf life is maintained. As a result, users can bypass the SLED entry.

Having said that your customizing is correct; the discrepancy is due to the Fiori app’s current logic. To force SLED mandatory in F1043, you need an enhancement (BAdI or app extension).

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA error: When sending an array of objects, it d...
entry.content:
The issue is likely due to a payload size or data structure limitation in SAP BPA or the OData action. Here’s how you can address it:

1. Check Payload Size Limits:
SAP BPA and OData services often have limits on the size of data you can send in a single request. Try reducing the number of documents or fields per request to see if the issue resolves.

2. Batch Processing:
Instead of sending all 100 documents at once, split your data into smaller batches (e.g., 20–30 documents per batch) and process them sequentially.

3. Suggest using automation to handle data splitting and loading in different automations.

entry.title: SAP Service Request FAQs
entry.content:
The Service Requests application offers the ability to request services for delivered systems, based on the SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS)  

Roles and Responsibilities (R&R) document. The service request catalogue includes services categorized as: 

 Standard services: non-chargeable and provided as part of the standard contract.  
Optional services: Subject to additional service fees and require a contractual change request.  
Chargeable services:  

  • Enhanced Managed Services (EMS): Supplemental set of services subject to additional fees for SAP HANA Enterprise Cloud (BYOL and Subscription) contracts, which could or could not be performed by customer.  
  • Additional Services: Supplemental set of services subject to additional fees for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, and SAP HANA Enterprise Cloud Advanced Edition contracts. 

 Access the application via SAP for Me > ECS Workspace > Click Create Service Requests   

 

Steps: 

1.Logon SAP for Me > Service & Support dashboard 

2.Go to Service Request tab https://me.sap.com/servicessupport/servicerequests 

3.Click on 'New Service Request' tab  

4.Select desired template >> Proceed to create 

5.Authorizations:  

6.Create Billable Service Requests     

  • Create Service Requests 
  • Display Service Requests 
  • Report Technical Problem  
  • (for template) - Note: If templates are missing, the authorization needs to be set at the customer number or CCOE level (not just installation number level) - see KBA 3463116. 
  • If unable to create SR or any issues during SR application - create a case for component XX-SER-FORME-ECS with relevant screenshots and details. 

Further details can be found on KBA 2720477 How to access ECS Service Requests - SAP for Me   

https://me.sap.com/notes/3239384/E- How to use Service Requests application - SAP for Me   
https://me.sap.com/notes/3432011/E 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3355416/E 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3462685 

3463116 - Missing template(s) when creating service request 

What is Service Request in SAP for Me? (check 2nd half of the blog) 

 Link for Service request creation: 

https://learning.sap.com/videos/creating-a-service-request-in-sap-for-me 

 You can also check this document that explains the use of Service Request: https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/my-support/help-for-sap-support-applic...    

 Guides: Video   

https://learning.sap.com/videos/using-a-service-request-application-in-sap-for-me 

 Thank you for reading!   

We hope you found this helpful.

entry.title: Re: Automatic deletion of unissued old reservations
entry.content:
Good day @Prathamesh1210 

Thank you for your question.

The Public Cloud does not offer automatic deletion of old expired reservations as a generic background cleanup process. You can use the Manage Manual Reservations app to analyse reservation status. The correct cleanup path is usually for you to complete or close the originating business document so that the reservation is flagged or cleared according to that process logic.

See the following references for more insights.

2494900 - Purchase Requisition items and reservation items are deleted when order is Technically Completed - SAP for Me

3673895 - Request a CDS view can be used to check reservation item details & status - SAP for Me

 

Therefore, my recommended best practice would be:

  • For you to clean up old manual reservations, make use of the Manage Manual Reservations app.

 

  • To analyse age, completion, and whether the reservation is still active, the Manage Reservation Items app will provide support.

  • For automatic reservations, you should resolve them through the originating business document lifecycle, not through a separate deletion batch job.
  • If your requirement is a true periodic housekeeping process for expired reservations that are older than a certain amount of time, no standard background job for such process is provided. You may consider raising an enhancement with SAP for that.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Does Fiori with same function :Accounts Document Outp...
entry.content:
Good day @yeziyimm8 

Thank you for your question.

There is no Japan-specific standard app that matches Accounts Document Output-China one to one. The approach I would recommend for Japan is to print the accounting document through Create Correspondence using SAP09, ideally launching it through Manage Journal Entries. You can achieve internal accounting voucher style output and is the closest standard alternative for journal entry printing.

See the following references for more insights.

3283519 - Print functionality for customer invoice - FI Document - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

3340336 - Printable forms for Journal Entry document. - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b8c08e0197454541a11f8d46ef1ab96e/b4dbea5796ae9d7fe10000000a154cd9.html?locale=en-US

 

You can test this in your system by:

  • Opening the Manage Journal Entries app,

 

  • Then open your posted document, choose Create Correspondence, enter SAP09 and preview the PDF document.

  • If the documents has no entry view, switch to SAP09 Ledger View and specify the ledger. If preview fails, check Output Parameter Determination and verify the form template assignment and correct identifier such as S09EN or S09LE.

See the following reference for more insights.

3417531 - Correspondence for internal documents does not show values for line items for Asset Posting. - SAP for Me

3716717 - Error "Output could not generate the requested PDF document" in Create Correspondence App – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Innovation Award 2026 – Who’s in for Utilities?
entry.content:
We’re excited to unveil the utility nominations of the 13th edition of the SAP Innovation Awards! This program honors organizations that use SAP solutions in creative and forward-thinking ways, enabling them to achieve outstanding results and adapt to the ever-changing needs of their customers.

A big congratulations to all utilities customers and partners who submitted entries this year. Your innovative use of SAP solutions to help the world run better is truly inspiring!

Let’s take a closer look at the inspiring projects showcased by our customers:

14 Submissions from Utility companies:

  • Anglian Water transformed its manual budget management process into a fully automated system using SAP technologies. This new approach ensures real-time visibility, improved forecasting accuracy, and agile decision-making, enhancing strategic insight and efficiency in managing a multi-billion-pound portfolio.

  • BC Hydro modernized its ERP and Asset Management systems with SAP S/4HANA, addressing technical debt and improving efficiency that enabled real-time insights, simplified user experiences, and digital-first operations, positioning it for reliable service and future innovation.

  • Cadent Gas In high-risk situations, emergency teams need clear, real-time visibility to respond effectively. The Dispatch Performance (ESOS) Dashboard consolidates gas leakage data into a single live view, enhancing response speed, operational efficiency, compliance, and public safety.

  • Centrica Plc deployed a governed data foundation with SAP Business AI, refocusing resources from manual reporting to data modeling, predictive forecasting, and scenario planning. This transformation enhances grid optimization, profitability, and operational agility, supporting the UK's energy transition.

  • Dominion Energy transformed its customer experience by unifying digital capabilities into a single intelligent ecosystem that improved customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, and scalability, supporting diverse customer segments with personalized, seamless experiences.

  • Engie's DCP unit modernized its 20-year-old SAP ECC IS-Utilities landscape by moving to SAP S/4HANA Utilities through a Shellfield conversion and cloud transition on AWS. This upgrade improved billing and customer care, boosted efficiency, and enabled faster innovation for a future-ready digital core.

  • Elektrizitätswerk des Kantons Schaffhausen AG (EKS) and AGILITA built a GenAI Mail Assistant on SAP BTP, connecting real-time energy data to customer service. This AI-driven solution reduces response times by 60-70%, improves accuracy, and provides consistent replies, transforming customer service efficiency for 44,000 Swiss households.
  • Loudoun Water modernized its enterprise applications with SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and SAP Service Cloud v2 to enhance reliability, reduce costs, and improve customer service. This transformation supports scalability, future-proofing capabilities, and faster onboarding, ensuring operational excellence and customer satisfaction.

  • Ontario Power Generation faced costly challenges from siloed enterprise architecture using SAP LeanIX, creating a transparent, self-service platform that aligned IT investments and reduced redundancies. This led to cost savings, better decision-making, and improved collaboration.

  • Oklahoma City Water Utilities Trust (OCWUT)  modernized its infrastructure by integrating SAP S/4HANA with a cloud platform, streamlining billing, account updates, and service workflows. This enhanced digital engagement, boosted self-service, and reduced operational effort for 1.4 million residents.

  • Severn Trent Water PLC every drop matters — not just in pipes and plants, but in the lives of the communities we serve. By re-engineering our integration landscape using SAP BTP, we achieved faster resolutions, seamless communication, and reliable customer experiences, transforming our operations and reinforcing trust in our services.

  • Southern California Edison(SCE) leverages SAP’s integrated transformation suite to digitize the entire lifecycle, boosting efficiency and enabling continuous improvement. This approach modernizes operations, enhances customer experience, and delivers enterprise-wide transparency.
  • The City of Tacoma leveraged AI-enabled SAP S/4HANA transformation to improve analytics reliability, reduce manual effort, and accelerate delivery. This modernization initiative minimized power outages, improved issue identification, and enhanced service delivery for nearly 220,000 residents, ensuring better outcomes for the community.
  • TransAlta Corporation modernized its ERP by moving from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud through a targeted Bluefield transformation, reducing customizations by over 55% while preserving key investments. This enabled a smooth go-live, improved efficiency, and created a scalable foundation for future innovation.

Thank you to all our customers for once again pushing the boundaries of innovation!
We’re keeping our fingers crossed for the finalist announcement on April 7 and the winner announcement on April 14.

But for us, you are all winners — tackling the challenges of the utilities industry and driving the transformation toward a new, innovative future. 🏆

entry.title: What is planned for the 2605 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing?
entry.content:
The 2605 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing is planned to be deployed to BTP customer tenants on April 22, 2026:

Administration UI and Administration service:

Release

Test/Non-Productive & Productive Upgrades

2605

April 22, 2026

 

Variant Configuration service & Pricing service:

Release

Test/Non-productive Upgrade

Productive Upgrade

2605

April 22, 2026

May 13, 2026

 

This blog covers the following planned innovations:

  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Sunset of /closeSession
  • Configuration and Pricing Services – New Data Center: SAP Cloud Infrastructure in Europe (Rot) (cf-eu02)
  • Configuration and Pricing Services – End of Support for AWS Data Center in Europe (Frankfurt) (cf-eu11)
  • Pricing service – Extension Concept – Allow Creation/Updating of Pricing Attributes from Extensions
  • Pricing service – Extension Concept – Allow accessDateList as Input to Extensions
  • Pricing service – Support Access Type B
  • Pricing service – Extension Concept – Allow Overwriting Condition Rate Unit from Custom Extensions
  • Variant Configuration service – Optimized Read Access for Large Variant Tables with NonAtomic Cells
  • Variant Configuration service – Search by Quote Id

 

It also includes a call to act regarding the following changes:

  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Sunset of /closeSession

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – Sunset of /closeSession

The endpoints POST /closeSession of the configuration and pricing services used to be used to remove configurations and pricing documents from the HTTP session. This is no longer required because of improved resource management.

The endpoints were set to be deprecated with release 2505 and are now removed with the release 2605 as announced in the past.

Action: If called by your application, remove those calls before the release in May 2026.

More information was published with the 2025 May release in the API definition of the Pricing service and the Variant Configuration service and in the Development Guide.

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – New Data Center: SAP Cloud Infrastructure in Europe (Rot) (cf-eu02)

SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing is available in multiple hyperscaler regions. For EU Access contracts, configuration and pricing services will be available exclusively in SAP Cloud Infrastructure – Europe (Rot) (cf-eu02).

All configuration and pricing tenants of a customer must be operated in one data center (no mixing providers/regions). It is not possible to switch data centers for existing tenants.

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – End of Support for AWS Data Center in Europe (Frankfurt) (cf-eu11)

For EU Access contracts, SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing will no longer support AWS Europe (Frankfurt) EU Access (cf-eu11). For non-EU Access contracts, AWS Europe (Frankfurt) (cf-eu10) continues to be supported. EU Access contracts must use SAP Cloud Infrastructure Europe (Rot) (cf-eu02) going forward.

 

Pricing service – Extension Concept – Allow Creation/Updating of Pricing Attributes from Extensions

Requirement formulas can now create and update pricing attributes at runtime. This enables powerful scenarios where pricing context is enriched dynamically and immediately consumed by subsequent condition evaluations.

Key capabilities

  • Create new pricing attributes within requirement formulas.
  • Update existing attributes within requirement formulas.
  • Changes take effect immediately for all subsequent pricing condition evaluations.

Available scopes

  • Item‑Level Scope – Make attributes available across all pricing conditions for the entire item.
  • Condition‑Level Scope (default) – Restrict attributes to a specific pricing condition evaluation.

More information will be published with the May release in the Extension Guide (Pre-Production).

 

Pricing service – Extension Concept – Allow accessDateList as Input to Extensions

Pricing formulas can now access date information (for example, pricing and billing dates) from pricing items to implement time based logic without custom data retrieval.

How it works

  1. In action collect_attributes, declare the needed data. For example: accessDateList in the itemInput projection.
  2. At runtime, the service retrieves the relevant dates and passes them to action process_formula.

Use cases for custom routines:

  • Time dependent pricing logic
  • Contract validity and billing checks

More information will be published with the May release in the Extension Guide (Pre-Production).

 

Pricing service – Support Access Type B

During condition finding, it is possible to fetch values for fields marked with access type B directly from the found condition record and reuse them in subsequent accesses.

Out-of-the-box support – Sales Deals

  • For the standard Sales Deal field (KNUMA_AG), requirement formula 62 retrieves the Sales Deal from the current condition and exposes it for subsequent condition determination (no additional implementation required anymore).

Extensibility – custom attributes

  • For attributes beyond Sales Deal, implement custom requirement formulas. Together with the capability to create/update pricing attributes via extensions, you can enrich additional attributes and use them in access sequence logic.

More information will be published with the May release in the Extension Guide (Pre-Production).

 

Pricing service – Extension Concept – Allow Overwriting Condition Rate Unit from Custom Extensions

Pricing base and value formulas can now dynamically update the condition unit during execution — enabling flexible, unit-based pricing (for example commodity scenarios that convert PC → KGM at runtime). The formula evaluates the context and returns an updated condition unit for the condition evaluation.

More information will be published with the May release in the Extension Guide (Pre-Production).

 

Variant Configuration service – Optimized Read Access for Large Variant Tables with Non‑Atomic Cells

Access to large variant tables with non‑atomic cells required unnecessary time in specific scenarios. The read logic has been optimized, significantly improving response times when reading such tables through the /configurations APIs.

 

Variant Configuration service – Search by Quote Id

You can now filter stored configurations by a quote ID — for example:

GET /externalConfigurations?$filter=sourceId LIKE "*<quoteId>*"

You can query configurations where the sourceId contains the quote id at the beginning, end, or anywhere in the string. This makes it easy to retrieve all configurations related to a quote that contains several items (as soon as the caller provides the quote- and item id in the sourceId when creating a configuration).

More information will be published with the May release in the API Definition and in the Development Guide (Pre-Production).

 

With that, you have a good overview of what innovations are planned for the May release. Please check the updated roadmap and release notes after the release date to see which of these features were delivered as planned.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud UM: Utilizing of Parameter model in Writer function
entry.content:
Friendly Reminder

A basic understanding of APIs may be helpful to fully appreciate the context of this technical blog post.

Hi Everyone

SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud UM) offers powerful writing capabilities that let you transmit payloads to external applications via Connection, whether to SAP Analytics Cloud models, SOAP services, or OData services.

Now, I am here to share a short blog post highlighting how you can seamlessly configure a Parameterized model using Connections on write enabled APIs.

Before we proceed, let me first provide some context on parameterized models that leverage write enabled APIs.

When an OData Service Connection is created in SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model, it defines all available set of fields and parameters that can be used during modeling covering both fields for reading and the required parameters for writing.

These available sets of fields and parameters are being converted as a Model Entity from a successful Connection creation. To give you an example, let’s use this SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SAP SCT) API which is /GetQuantitativeMetricData.

For more information about this API, please see SAP SCT Analytical Use Case.

Using this GetQuantitativeMetricData POST request in SAP PaPM Cloud UM Connection will look like the image below. To have a better understanding, I have also added a brief description for each sections that are highlighted in the image.

  1. This was identified based from the SAP SCT Analytical Use Case which the POST request endpoint is GetQuantitativeMetricData .
  2. When you create the OData Service connection, SAP PaPM Cloud UM reads the $metadata and understands the fixed structure if the POST request requires Parameter Model.

    Therefore, the OData Service connection created will be defining the Parameter Model in its column.

  3. Once you have the details from the Parameter Model, you can identify what the Parameter Model is and then proceed to use this model in the connection by filling in the required fields.

Note: All of the Models from the image below are Sync Model and Connection as models from this endpoint GetQuantitativeMetricData are all required and contains relation.

After identifying all the required inputs and outputs, you can proceed with configuring the Writer function. Please note that the Writer function accepts a parameterized model only when the Model endpoint GetQuantitativeMetricData is used. Otherwise, the Writer function will detect the mismatch, and you will not be able to save the configuration. See image below:

But when you use the Parameter model as an input, since the Parameter Model is already defined from the creation of Connection, Writer function will not require you to perform a Field mapping.

If in any case you have accidentally mapped a field, then you will receive an information like the image below.

Once configured, you may now proceed on performing the Run of your Writer function with the calculated data records you have Written / Upload to Parameterized Model.

That concludes this blog post. I hope this helped demonstrate how SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model intelligently recognizes OData service structures, making configuration easier and more user‑friendly.

Thank you so much! Bye for now!

entry.title: Enhancing Your SAP Digital Manufacturing with Continuous Quality Checks
entry.content:
Implementing and operating SAP Digital Manufacturing is an important step in strengthening manufacturing processes, but it can also introduce challenges that may affect system stability and efficiency. To help address these concerns, we introduce the Continuous Quality Check (CQC) services for SAP Digital Manufacturing.
 
Continuous quality check (CQC) services are remote services embedded in your SAP Digital Manufacturing subscription and offered as part of your SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition maintenance contract at no extra cost.

Continuous Quality Checks for SAP Digital Manufacturing

Below is an overview of the CQC services available for SAP Digital Manufacturing.
 

Service

Benefit

CQC for Deployment Readiness 

Evaluates your SAP Digital Manufacturing tenant configuration and provides recommendations aligned with SAP best practices to support operational efficiency and a smoother transition to production. Evaluation includes SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Digital Manufacturing, and Project Management and Operational Support.

This service is ideal before go‑live for new implementation or additional rollout phases.

CQC for Solution Optimization

Performs a health check of your productive SAP Digital Manufacturing tenant. Evaluation includes SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Digital Manufacturing, and Project Management and Operational Support.

It identifies potential risks and improvement opportunities and provides SAP best‑practice recommendations as part of your post‑go‑live activities.

 

What's In It For You

  • A comprehensive view of your system configuration.
  • Safeguarding your upcoming go-live or ensuring your system health after go-live.
  • Avoiding common pitfalls that could impact performance and stability.


The Outcome

You will receive a service report that includes a detailed analysis of areas for improvement, a prioritized action plan and recommendations.

Why This Matters

Whether preparing for your first go‑live or optimizing your productive environment, CQC services deliver insights that help you proactively address potential issues, stabilize performance, and support continuous improvement.  Evaluation includes SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Digital Manufacturing, and Project Management and Operational Support.

For detailed guidance on how these continuous quality check services support system stability and risk mitigation, refer to our knowledge article, Optimizing SAP Digital Manufacturing Operations with Continuous Quality Checks.

Access the knowledge article today!


Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps

Beyond continuous quality check, SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. Value maps on SAP Community bring together guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next - all included in your SAP cloud subscription.

👉  Watch our short demo video, then head over to our page to explore our range of topics and request a call with a topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals.  

 

About the author: 

Mabelle Roque Miguel is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in SAP Supply Chain Management solutions. Based in Singapore, Mabelle has been with SAP since 2007, taking on diverse roles that have largely centered around customer engagement and success. Now, her focus is on guiding businesses through their supply chain journey, helping them accelerate the adoption of SAP supply chain management solutions with value map content and SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

💡  Have questions or feedback on this content? Feel free to leave a comment below or submit them in the Q&A section.

entry.title: Re: he supplier down payment app is currently not visible...
entry.content:
Hello @paresh31837,

to your additional remark: "It works only if you are using MM-SD module, We want to post FI down payment only since we are not using MM-SD."

According to the App Library and the Process Navigator description it should be possible even if you only have accounting scope items J59 / J60 activated.

Unfortunately, I can not report from my actual knowledge, since we have FI as well as MM-SD scope items activated in our system.

However, these links show it should be possible with just FI scope items J59 / J60:

 

1) SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library F1688

 

2) Process Navigator J59 - SAP for Me: "also" implies both ways are possible, via J59 or via BJK (SD)

 

 

 

If you have those accounting scope items J59 / J60 activated and Down Payment is still not possible, I would suggest creating a support case with SAP directly at SAP for Me. Maybe they can give you a definite answer 🙂

 

Best regards,

Kerisha
